title: willughbeia edulis
intro: Willughbeia edulis, also known as the "tiger's breast," is a rare and endangered plant species native to the island of New Caledonia. The plant is a member of the Apocynaceae family and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Pacific Island medicine. The leaves and roots of Willughbeia edulis are rich in alkaloids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, have been used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to support digestive health.
